response: IT IS Essential TO MAINTAIN GOOD ORAL HYGIENE AND TAKE PRECAUTIONARY MEASURES UNTIL YOU CAN SEE A DENTIST WHEN YOU ARE IN A SITUATION WHERE YOU CANNOT IMMEDIATELY GET DENTAL CARE. KEEP THE AREA CLEAN BY BRUSHING GENTLY AND THOROUGHLY AT LEAST TWICE A DAY WITH A SOFT-BRISTLED TOOTHBRUSH. AFTER EVERY MEAL, RINSE YOUR MOUTH WITH WATER TO REMOVE ANY FOOD PARTICLES THAT MIGHT GET LODGED IN THE CAVITY. FLOSS CAREFULLY AROUND THE AFFECTED TOOTH TO REMOVE ANY FOOD PARTICLES, BUT BE GENTLE TO AVOID CAUSING MORE DAMAGE OR PAIN. USE TEMPORARY FILLING MATERIALS FROM PHARMACIES TO FILL THE VOID TEMPORARILY AND PROTECT THE TOOTH UNTIL YOU CAN SEE A DENTIST. PAIN MANAGEMENT CAN BE DONE BY USING OVER-THE-COUNTER PAIN RELIEVERS LIKE IBUPROFEN OR ACETAMINOPHEN, BUT AVOID ASPIRIN WHICH CAN CAUSE BLEEDING IF APPLIED DIRECTLY TO THE GUMS OR THE CAVITY. SALTWATER RINSE TO REDUCE INFLAMMATION AND BACTERIA BY MIXING ABOUT 1/2 TEASPOON OF SALT IN 8 OUNCES OF WARM WATER AND RINSE YOUR MOUTH GENTLY. AVOID EXTREMES BY CHWING ON THE OPPOSITE SIDE OF YOUR MOUTH TO AVOID PUTTING PRESSURE ON THE DAMAGED TOOTH. MONITOR FOR CHANGES BY KEEPING AN EYE ON THE TOOTH FOR ANY CHANGES OR SIGNS OF INFECTION INCLUDING INCREASED PAIN, SENSITIVITY, SWELLING, OR A BAD TASTE IN THE MOUTH. SEEK DENTAL CARE EVEN IF IT IS DIFFICULT BY LOOKING FOR DENTAL CARE OPTIONS IN YOUR CURRENT LOCATION DENTAL SCHOOLS, EXPATRIATE COMMUNITIES, OR LOCAL HEALTHCARE SERVICES CAN OFTEN PROVIDE GUIDANCE ON WHERE TO FIND RELIABLE DENTAL CARE. DENTAL EMERGENCIES CAN BE DEALT WITH BY SEEKING DENTAL TREATMENT IMMEDIATELY IF YOU DEVELOP SIGNS OF A DENTAL ABSCESS SUCH AS SEVERE PAIN,
